task_completion.description: Configure segments for Travel, Mystery, and Poetry with correct URL patterns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screaming Frog 'Configuration > Segments' window showing three segments created: 'Travel' (matching 'travel_2'), 'Mystery' (matching 'mystery_3'), and 'Poetry' (matching 'poetry_23').
Screaming Frog main interface showing 'https://books.toscrape.com/' in the URL bar, with the crawl progress bar active or completed at 100%.

task_completion.description: Verify segments are applied to the crawl results.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screaming Frog right-hand pane or main window showing the 'Segments' tab with populated crawl data for the Travel, Mystery, and Poetry categories.

task_completion.description: Export the internal HTML crawl results to the specified CSV path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screaming Frog export dialog saving to '~/Documents/SEO/exports/segmented_crawl.csv', OR a file manager/terminal showing the 'segmented_crawl.csv' file successfully created in that directory.

task_completion.description: Generate a summary report documenting the total page counts for each segment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displaying the contents of '~/Documents/SEO/reports/segment_counts.txt', which explicitly lists the three segments (Travel, Mystery, Poetry) alongside their respective page counts.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Screaming Frog SEO Spider application to perform the crawl.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Screaming Frog GUI being interacted with, rather than terminal scripts, Python scrapers, or alternative tools.

integrity.description: The exported CSV appears to be a genuine Screaming Frog export.
integrity.visual_evidence: The export process is clearly visible via the Screaming Frog UI, or if the CSV is opened, it shows standard Screaming Frog columns (e.g., Address, Content, Status Code).

integrity.description: The summary report contains actual numbers derived from the crawl.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'segment_counts.txt' file contains specific numerical counts for each category (e.g., ~11-12 for Travel, ~32-33 for Mystery, ~19-20 for Poetry), not placeholder text or copy-pasted prompt instructions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target URL: https://books.toscrape.com/. Required segments and patterns: Travel (travel_2), Mystery (mystery_3), Poetry (poetry_23). Export path: /home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/segmented_crawl.csv. Report path: /home/ga/Documents/SEO/reports/segment_counts.txt. Expected approximate counts based on website data (books + category index pages): Travel (~11-12), Mystery (~32-33), Poetry (~19-20).
